scope.summary: Normalize launch adapters for local, cloud, CLI, human, subagent, and validator lanes.
forbidden_paths: direct model endpoint calls outside LlmClient
npm/JavaScript proof targets for this WP
app/src-tauri launch or IPC authority for this WP
proof commands that pass with zero matching tests
acceptance_criteria: Local lanes launch through LlmClient -> ModelRuntime.
Cloud lanes launch through existing CloudLane/BYOK boundary.
CLI, human/operator, validator, and subagent lanes have normalized records and startup failure status.

All local/cloud/CLI/human/subagent/validator lane launches MUST resolve through the Rust backend SwarmCoordinator/ModelRuntime adapter registry; LlmClient may be used only as an existing service facade when it delegates to that registry. No frontend, app/src, direct endpoint, or terminal-only path may become launch authority.

Launch adapter output records capability negotiation results, provider feature profile, requested/effective execution policy, trace_id/span_id, owner_session, reclaim policy, cancellation token boundary, and startup failure code for every lane kind.

Local OpenAI-compatible servers such as llama.cpp and Rust-native runtimes such as Candle are adapter backends only; no MT may call them as authoritative side channels outside the ModelRuntime boundary.
Tool-capable lanes route tool calls through capability check, consent/projection where needed, Flight Recorder/EventLedger logging, execution, result capture, and ModelLaneMessage result refs.

Every lane kind (local, BYOK cloud, official CLI, CLI bridge, human/operator, subagent, validator) MUST declare cancellation_token_ref, reclaim_policy, owner_session, terminal status mapping, and EventLedger START/STOP or explicit no-OS-process equivalent at launch time.
Source/negative proof MUST fail closed for direct endpoint launch, app/src/app-src-tauri launch authority, terminal-only launch state, and unsupported tool capability.

After implementation and before READY_FOR_VALIDATION, this MT MUST receive post-implementation adversarial review against the actual diff. Findings must be fixed or converted into explicit lifecycle blocker/missing_resource/proof_status state; the implementer may not self-certify COMPLETED.

REOPENED SCOPE (operator decision 2026-07-01): The default LlmClient boot path (src/backend/handshake_core/src/main.rs) MUST resolve local inference through the embedded ModelRuntime (LlamaCppRuntime default, CandleRuntime for hook/non-transformer lanes), NOT the auto-detected Ollama daemon. src/backend/handshake_core/src/llm/ollama.rs OllamaAdapter and its /api/tags auto-detect default are removed.

REOPENED SCOPE: A missing or unconfigured local model fails closed to DisabledLlmClient with no daemon fallback; a negative test proves the default runtime path never selects a third-party daemon adapter as authority (master-spec-v02.197 3.6.1).

REOPENED SCOPE (feature design hardened by pre-impl review): Candle (pure-Rust, CPU) is the compiled-in default embedded engine via a new `local-inference-default = ["candle-runtime-engine"]` feature added to Cargo `default`. llama.cpp (`llama-cpp-runtime-engine`, heavy C++ `llama-cpp-sys-2`) stays OPT-IN and MUST NOT be added to `default` (it breaks the RC-004 non-target `cargo check` and forces a C++ compile on every proof run). GPU backends (candle-cuda/metal, llama-cpp-cuda/metal/vulkan) are declared opt-in features, never in `default`, not built under load. Engine enablement is decoupled from app/src-tauri (remove `candle-runtime-engine` from app/src-tauri/Cargo.toml; the engine comes from handshake_core's own default). 'CPU-only baseline' means the enabled engine selects the CPU backend; GPU is a separate opt-in.

REOPENED SCOPE: The generic ExternalEngineImport OpenAI-compatible endpoint is retained as a non-authoritative compat lane only (provider=external_compat, not Handshake-owned in ProcessOwnershipLedger); no bespoke Ollama adapter or auto-wiring survives.

REOPENED SCOPE (spec MUSTs per master-spec-v02.197 3.6.2/4.6.1): the embedded default engine load path MUST box under a SandboxAdapter (default HandshakeNativeSandboxAdapter) and emit ProcessOwnershipLedger START/STOP rows; implementer verifies the existing ModelRuntime load path already satisfies this or wires it, else records a blocking HBR gap (a 5.6 HBR-gate failure if missed).

REOPENED SCOPE (spec MUSTs per 4.2.3.2(3)/4.2.3.1.1 + CX-101/CX-102): every completion still emits a Flight Recorder event with trace_id/model_id/TokenUsage on ALL default paths (embedded ModelRuntime success, DisabledLlmClient fail-closed, external_compat); the retained OpenAI-compatible compat lane stays mediated by LlmClient with no leftover direct HTTP from the deleted ollama.rs; max_tokens budget enforcement (BudgetExceeded) survives the swap.

REOPENED SCOPE (proof hardening per pre-impl review): the prior 14 lane proof targets exercise only swarm_orchestration::model_lane and DO NOT touch the Ollama-kill path (they can pass while main.rs still builds OllamaAdapter). The reopened MT requires NEW engine-free proof: (a) a negative test that the default LlmClient boot resolution with no local model returns DisabledLlmClient and never an Ollama/daemon adapter, and that init_llm_client/ProviderRegistry no longer has an OllamaAdapter arm; (b) a positive test that the generic external_compat OpenAI-compatible lane is retained and non-authoritative; (c) `cargo check --features app-runtime` proving main.rs compiles. Refactor init_llm_client's provider resolution into a testable function. The prior 14 proofs count as prior-scope evidence only.

REOPENED SCOPE (scope guard): MT-003 WIRES the default LlmClient to the already-shipped WP-KERNEL-004 ModelRuntime adapters (LlamaCppRuntime/CandleRuntime); it does NOT re-implement engines. Real embedded-engine inference (real GGUF through llama.cpp / real weights through Candle) is recorded DEFERRED-with-reason (heavy host-toolchain build under heavy load) citing tests/llama_cpp_e2e_smoke.rs and tests/candle_e2e_smoke.rs as the unfaked reproduction commands; it MUST NOT be faked green.

REOPENED SCOPE (wiring approach per pre-impl review): the embedded-runtime LlmClient bridge already exists (llm/local_router.rs: LocalRouter + LocalModelRuntimeLlmClient, LlmClient-implementing, LlamaCpp-default/Candle-for-hooks, currently constructed only in tests). MT-003 does NOT add a new LlmClient impl; it (a) adds a net-new local-model config surface to llm/registry.rs from_env (GGUF artifact_path + sha256 + RuntimeBinding + display name); (b) in main.rs init_llm_client replaces the ProviderKind::Ollama auto-detect arm with a local-runtime arm that builds LlamaCppRuntime+CandleRuntime, loads+registers the artifact (minting a UUIDv7 ModelId, provider=Local, matching binding; ModelRegistry.register requires non-empty artifact_path + non-zero sha256; validate_binding forbids LlamaCpp+activation_steering so steering lanes use Candle), constructs LocalRouter + LocalModelRuntimeLlmClient with a DisabledLlmClient fallback; (c) fails closed to DisabledLlmClient AT INIT (no /api/tags probe, no daemon fallback) when no local model is configured or load() fails (do not hard-abort startup, mirror existing degradations at main.rs:396/407/439).

REOPENED SCOPE (HIGH regression guard 1 - profile.model_id identity): the wired client's profile().model_id MUST be the minted UUIDv7 of the registered model, because call sites pass profile().model_id as CompletionRequest.model_id and LocalModelRuntimeLlmClient::completion only routes to the runtime for UUIDv7 ids (non-UUID silently falls back to the DisabledLlmClient fallback). A test MUST prove completion routes to the embedded runtime, not the fallback.

REOPENED SCOPE (HIGH regression guard 2 - embedding not dropped): OllamaAdapter implemented embedding(); LocalModelRuntimeLlmClient inherits the trait default embedding()->EmbeddingUnsupported. The swap MUST wire LlmClient::embedding() to ModelRuntime::embed() in llm/local_router.rs (or record an explicit operator waiver), else semantic embeddings silently drop and LoomSearchV2 degrades to keyword/trigram. A test MUST prove embedding() is not silently unsupported after the swap.

SCOPE ACCURACY (post-review): 'kill' is LlmClient-DEFAULT-scoped, not repo-wide. The OllamaAdapter type + InMemoryLlmClient are retained ONLY as test doubles (never constructed in the default path; no ProviderKind::Ollama variant remains). Ollama couplings intentionally remain OUTSIDE the default LlmClient path and are NOT part of MT-003: tokenization.rs OllamaTokenizerConfigCache, atelier ollama: model-server scheme, bin/capability_registry_workflow.rs OLLAMA_MODEL, dependency_policy tripwires. Removing those is out of MT-003 scope; if desired, a future WP/MT should own it.
proof_targets: REOPENED (engine-free, --features test-utils): negative test proving init_llm_client provider resolution with no local model configured returns DisabledLlmClient and never an Ollama/daemon adapter, and that ProviderRegistry/init_llm_client no longer expose an OllamaAdapter default arm (implementer names the exact test; must fail on zero matching tests).

REOPENED (engine-free, --features test-utils): test proving the default resolves through LocalModelRuntimeLlmClient with profile().model_id = the registered UUIDv7 and completion routes to the ModelRuntime (fake runtime) rather than the fallback.
REOPENED (engine-free, --features test-utils): test proving LlmClient::embedding() remains supported via ModelRuntime::embed() after the swap (or an explicit recorded operator waiver).
REOPENED (engine-free, --features test-utils): test proving the generic external_compat OpenAI-compatible lane (OpenAiCompatAdapter) is retained and non-authoritative.
REOPENED: cargo check --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features app-runtime (proves main.rs/init_llm_client compiles; the pg_test suites never build the bin).

REOPENED DEFERRED-with-reason (heavy host-toolchain build under heavy load; NOT faked green): real embedded inference via cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features "test-utils,candle-runtime-engine" --test candle_e2e_smoke, and --features "test-utils,llama-cpp-runtime-engine" --test llama_cpp_e2e_smoke.
scope.risk_if_missed: Local and cloud models remain separate launch systems and cannot be reasoned about by the coordinator.

implementation_notes: Local model lanes must launch through LlmClient -> ModelRuntime; direct local endpoint calls outside LlmClient are a failure.
Cloud model lanes must launch through existing CloudLane/BYOK adapter boundaries and surface ProjectionPlan/ConsentReceipt requirements to MT-006.
CLI bridge lanes must be represented as ModelLane records with ProcessOwnershipLedger ownership and no opaque terminal-only state.
Subagent lanes must have stable lane_id, session_id, owner_session, claim/reclaim metadata, and startup failure states.
Human/operator and validator lanes may have null model_id but still need role, session, wp/mt, status, and message identity.
Launch tests must prove quiet operation: hidden/headless by default, no focus stealing, no blank terminal windows, reclaim on failure/cancel/staleness.
MCP-style tool/resource concepts translate to Handshake ToolGate plus typed lane messages; tool descriptions and provider claims are untrusted until capability/consent checks pass.
Every launch path must be cancellable, bounded, and reclaimable; deterministic failure records beat retry loops that only exist in terminal output.
Cloud and local lanes may share OpenAI-compatible request shapes, but Handshake authority is the Rust adapter record and EventLedger/ArtifactStore materialization, not the provider endpoint.
Use Flight Recorder/EventLedger payloads as bounded events plus artifact refs; do not put unbounded model output, raw secrets, or prompt-only state in event payloads.

If this MT changes any product behavior, storage/event contract, command, IPC/API path, diagnostic surface, model lane, or navigation path, update the in-product UserManual in the same implementation change per HBR-MAN-004.

Product implementation for this MT must be Rust-only: Rust backend plus Rust native egui/Argus surfaces where UI is touched. Legacy app/src, app/src-tauri, React, Tauri/WebView, TypeScript, JavaScript, docs/model-manual, and npm proof targets are reference-only or forbidden and MUST NOT satisfy implementation/proof authority.

Proof commands must name exact Rust test targets or exact test names. Zero matching tests is a hard failure. Deterministic provider fakes are allowed for cloud/local model calls, but PostgreSQL/EventLedger/ArtifactStore/CRDT/UserManual/Argus authority must touch real Handshake-owned Rust resources or an explicit real PostgreSQL URL.
Each behavior-changing MT must include same-change UserManual coverage for the changed behavior; MT-011 audits coverage and cannot substitute for same-change HBR-MAN-004 proof.

This pre-implementation contract review does not replace post-implementation review. After coding, run adversarial review against the actual diff through the listed lenses and update MT status signaling before handoff.

The live Rust Tauri backend request-source path was patched only to close a Dexterity launch-authority bypass: app/src-tauri IPC and scheduled spin-up now attach core Dexterity launch contracts before SwarmCoordinator dispatch and fail closed when PostgreSQL ModelLaneStore is unavailable. This does not make Tauri/app IPC launch authority for this WP.

REOPENED 2026-07-01: MT-003 was returned from READY_FOR_VALIDATION to PENDING because the default LlmClient boot wiring (main.rs) still selects the OllamaAdapter daemon client, which bypasses ModelRuntime and contradicts this MT's own acceptance. The prior swarm-lane launch hardening (Dexterity bypass closures) remains valid prior-scope evidence; the reopened scope adds default-LlmClient embedded-ModelRuntime resolution, OllamaAdapter removal, CPU-baseline engine features, and retention of the non-authoritative ExternalEngineImport compat lane. The pre-existing external spec-manifest drift on 04-llm-infrastructure.md must also be resolved.

risks: Adapter-specific launch semantics can leak into higher-level routing.
minimum_controls: Normalize every launch into ModelLane records and test failed/cancelled launches.

research_basis.authority_posture: Master spec/SPEC_CURRENT/product code are authority; HANDSHAKE_PRODUCT_REFERENCE.md and external research are navigation/evidence only.
product_code_evidence: src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_runtime/trait.rs defines ModelRuntime and CancellationToken-aware runtime calls
src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_runtime/llama_cpp/ and candle/ exist for local runtime adapters

external_sources_checked.name: Hugging Face Candle Rust ML runtime

patterns_applied: Typed multi-agent orchestration becomes ModelLaneRun/ModelLane/ModelLaneMessage records, never peer-to-peer chat authority.
Durable workflow/replay patterns become PostgreSQL/EventLedger aggregates, checkpoints, projections, and deterministic state transitions.
CRDT research becomes explicit draft/proposal/update/state-vector/snapshot/stale-base records before promotion.
Trace/span research becomes propagated trace_id/span_id/model_session_id/lane_id correlation across lanes, tools, providers, artifacts, and diagnostics.
Tool protocol research becomes Handshake capability check, consent/projection, Flight Recorder/EventLedger logging, execution, result capture, and recovery.
rejected_options: peer-to-peer model channels outside coordinator-owned ModelLaneMessage records
legacy app/src React/Tauri code as runtime authority
SQLite runtime/test/fixture/cache/fallback authority for this WP
Flight Recorder-only replay without PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority rows
manual or markdown prose as product runtime authority
research_basis.selected_handshake_translation: Local/cloud/CLI/human/subagent/validator launches must normalize through Rust SwarmCoordinator/ModelRuntime adapter registry with capability/consent checks, quiet process ownership, cancellation, and deterministic startup failure states.

handshake_compatibility.product_reference_file_posture: reference/navigation only; do not override SPEC_CURRENT, master spec, build rules, or product code evidence
handshake_compatibility.proposal_promotion_model: Models may propose, critique, summarize, and produce artifacts; Handshake validates/promotes deterministically through Rust backend authority surfaces before state becomes authoritative.
durable_authority: CRDT/write-box when shared editable state is touched
observability_required: HBR-INT-009 tier posture for Flight Recorder, internal_diagnostics, Palmistry
handshake_compatibility.no_context_model_requirement: A model with no chat history must be able to implement and verify this MT from this contract, SPEC_CURRENT/master spec, product code paths, UserManual/backend navigation, and listed proof targets.
handshake_compatibility.rust_only_implementation: Rust backend plus Rust native egui/Argus only. TypeScript/React/app/src/app-src-tauri/npm/docs-model-manual are not implementation or proof authority for this WP.
handshake_compatibility.spec_realism_gate: Implementer may move only to READY_FOR_VALIDATION after exact Rust proof targets touch the named real resources; COMPLETED is validator-only.
